analysis.summary: Release 1.13.5 addresses several issues related to job backfills, asset checks, and UI improvements, enhancing the overall stability and usability of Dagster. The default SQLite `busy_timeout` has been increased to better handle slow filesystems, which may improve performance in certain environments. Additionally, the removal of deprecated support for older versions of papermill ensures compatibility with the latest features, while the introduction of new flags and error message improvements aids in debugging and operational efficiency.
key_changes: [SqliteEventLogStorage] Increased default `busy_timeout` from 5s to 30s to improve tolerance for slow filesystems.
[dagster-k8s] Added `delete_pod_on_completion` flag to `PipesK8sClient.run`, allowing users to control pod deletion behavior post-run.
[dagstermill] Dropped support for `papermill` 1.x, now requiring `papermill>=2.0.0`, which may affect users relying on older versions.
[Job Backfills] Renamed `DAGSTER_MAX_ASSET_BACKFILL_RETRIES` to `DAGSTER_MAX_BACKFILL_RETRIES` for consistency, with a fallback for backward compatibility.
[Asset Checks] Fixed a bug where asset checks could be dropped from a `JobDefinition` during subset selection, ensuring integrity of asset checks.
[Partitioned Assets] Resolved a regression where `OutputContext.asset_partition_key_range` could raise errors for assets using `MultiPartitionsDefinition` with `DynamicPartitionsDefinition`.
[UI] Improved error messaging for unavailable instances when loading dynamic partitions, enhancing user experience.
[dagster-dbt] Fixed runtime selection arguments not being applied correctly with `@dbt_assets`, ensuring accurate asset selection.
breaking_changes: Renamed environment variable `DAGSTER_MAX_ASSET_BACKFILL_RETRIES` to `DAGSTER_MAX_BACKFILL_RETRIES`, requiring updates to environment configurations.
analysis.migration_notes: Update any environment configurations to use `DAGSTER_MAX_BACKFILL_RETRIES` instead of the old name to ensure continued functionality.

analysis.summary: Release 1.13.4 introduces several enhancements and bug fixes that improve the usability and functionality of Dagster. Notably, it adds support for `typing.Mapping` and `typing.Sequence` annotations on op and asset inputs, enhancing type safety. The addition of a `path_prefix` parameter for `DagsterGraphQLClient` allows for better integration with webserver deployments behind non-root paths, which is crucial for users with specific deployment configurations. Overall, these changes aim to streamline development workflows and improve error handling for asset definitions.
key_changes: [op/asset inputs] Added support for `typing.Mapping` and `typing.Sequence` annotations, improving type safety and clarity in input definitions.
[DagsterGraphQLClient] Introduced a `path_prefix` parameter to support deployments behind non-root paths, enhancing flexibility in deployment configurations.
[TableMetadataSet] Added a `storage_kind` field to specify the underlying storage system for tables, aiding in better metadata management.
[define_asset_job] Improved validation for owner strings at definition time, providing clearer error messages for invalid inputs.
[dagster-azure] Added `endpoint_suffix` parameter for ADLS2 and Blob Storage utilities, enabling compatibility with various Azure cloud environments.
[dagster-dbt] Introduced a `translation` parameter in `DbtCloudComponent` for customizing dbt model translations, enhancing integration capabilities.
[dagster-soda] Launched a new `dagster-soda` library with `SodaScanComponent` for executing data quality checks, expanding Dagster's functionality.
[bug fixes] Resolved issues with asset checks and path-based dbt selectors, improving reliability and correctness in asset management.

analysis.summary: This release addresses a specific issue with Docker OS dependency installation, ensuring smoother integration for users relying on Docker environments. Additionally, it caps the dbt-adapters version to below 1.24 to prevent a KeyError that arises when older versions of dbt-core encounter changes in the JS UDF macro. This change is crucial for maintaining compatibility and stability for downstream consumers using these adapters.
key_changes: [Docker Integration] Fixed an issue with Docker OS dependency installation, which improves the reliability of dbt-core in containerized environments.
[Dependency Management] Capped dbt-adapters version to <1.24 to prevent KeyError during dbt parse, ensuring compatibility with existing dbt-core versions.

analysis.summary: The dbt-core v1.11.10 release addresses compatibility issues with dbt-adapters version 1.24, specifically related to JavaScript User Defined Functions (UDFs). By pinning dbt-adapters to a version below 1.24, this release prevents KeyErrors that could arise when older dbt-core versions encounter changes in the JS UDF macro. This ensures stability for downstream consumers relying on dbt-core and dbt-adapters integration.
key_changes: [Dependencies] Pinned dbt-adapters to <1.24 to avoid breaking changes introduced in dbt-adapters 1.24, which affects JS UDFs. This change is crucial for maintaining compatibility and preventing runtime errors.
[Dependencies] Capped dbt-adapters to <1.24 to prevent KeyError during dbt parse operations when using older dbt-core versions with the new JS UDF macro changes.

analysis.summary: Release v1.11.9 addresses specific bugs related to static analysis configuration and resource type detection, enhancing the reliability of the dbt-core tool. The update also includes improvements to deprecation warnings for macro configurations, ensuring clearer guidance for developers. Additionally, the Docker image dependencies have been updated to resolve build failures, which is crucial for maintaining a stable development environment for downstream consumers.
key_changes: [static_analysis] Fixed the interpretation of 'static_analysis: off' in manifest.json, ensuring it is treated as the string 'off' rather than a boolean false, which could lead to misconfigurations.
[state] Resolved an issue where state:modified was not detecting changes in .yml properties for resource_type:function, improving the accuracy of state tracking.
[jsonschemas] Updated jsonschemas to provide more accurate deprecation warnings, specifically ensuring that macro.config does not trigger unnecessary warnings.

[Docker] Bumped libpq-dev in the Docker image from version 13.23-0+deb11u1 to 13.23-0+deb11u2 and then to 13.23-0+deb11u3 to fix build failures due to superseded package versions, ensuring a smoother build process.

analysis.summary: Release 1.13.3 introduces several enhancements and bug fixes that improve the usability and reliability of Dagster jobs and UI components. Notably, the addition of the `owners` parameter to `define_asset_job` aligns asset jobs with regular jobs, enhancing metadata management. Improved error messages for missing configurations will aid developers in debugging, while the new ClickHouse libraries expand data integration capabilities. Overall, these changes enhance developer experience and system robustness without introducing breaking changes.
key_changes: [define_asset_job] Added an `owners` parameter to improve asset job metadata management.
[error handling] Enhanced error messages for missing required config in Dagster jobs, providing clearer guidance and example configurations.
[ui] Implemented client-side validation in the YAML editor for deployment settings to catch unknown keys early.
[dagster-clickhouse] Introduced new libraries (`dagster-clickhouse`, `dagster-clickhouse-pandas`, `dagster-clickhouse-polars`) for native ClickHouse integration.
[dagster-cloud] Updated `EcsUserCodeLauncher` to accept `repository_credentials` for ECR configuration at the agent or deployment level.
[dagster-postgres] Added workload identity federation (WIF) authentication options for PostgreSQL, enhancing security and flexibility in cloud environments.
[dagster-dbt] Fixed an issue with duplicate dependency entries in dbt assets, improving data integrity.
[dagster-snowflake] Replaced deprecated Pydantic `validator` with `field_validator` to eliminate warnings and ensure compatibility with future Pydantic versions.

analysis.summary: Release 1.13.2 addresses several bugs and introduces new features that enhance user experience and improve integration capabilities. Notably, the addition of the `credentials_strategy` argument in `DatabricksClientResource` allows for more flexible authentication flows. The removal of the `dg utils integrations` sub-command simplifies the command structure, while various UI fixes enhance usability. Overall, this release improves stability and functionality for downstream consumers relying on Dagster's core and library features.
key_changes: [dagster-databricks] Added `credentials_strategy` argument to `DatabricksClientResource`, enabling custom authentication flows, which enhances integration with Databricks SDK.
[dg] Removed `dg utils integrations` sub-command, streamlining the command-line interface and reducing potential confusion.
[ui] Introduced Cmd/Ctrl click functionality for launching runs in new tabs, improving user workflow without losing context.
[ui] Fixed CSS variable name mismatch that caused incorrect font rendering, ensuring consistent UI appearance.
[dagster-dbt] Resolved thread-safety issue in concurrent metadata fetching, reducing the likelihood of intermittent errors during metadata operations.
[dagster-snowflake-polars] Corrected dynamic partition key escaping, preventing potential errors in partition management.
[ui] Enhanced run config editor dialog height, improving usability for configuration tasks.
[dagster-cloud-cli] Fixed GitHub Enterprise authentication for branch deployment workflows, ensuring reliable CI/CD integration.
breaking_changes: [dg] The removal of the `dg utils integrations` sub-command may require users to adjust their command usage if they were relying on this functionality.
analysis.migration_notes: Update any scripts or documentation that reference the `dg utils integrations` sub-command to remove or replace it with alternative commands.

analysis.summary: Release 1.13.1 addresses several critical issues, including a SQL injection vulnerability in IO managers, which necessitates immediate upgrades for all users. The introduction of `PipesCompositeMessageReader` enhances the handling of concurrent message streams, while improvements in asset selection syntax and UI performance streamline user interactions. Additionally, state-backed integration components now default to a more efficient local filesystem storage, improving performance and reliability for downstream consumers.
key_changes: [PipesCompositeMessageReader] Added to support multiple concurrent message streams, enhancing the capability of Pipes sessions.
[Asset Selection Syntax] Introduced support for `sensor:`, `schedule:`, and `job:` attributes, allowing more granular asset queries.
[State Storage] State-backed integration components now default to `LOCAL_FILESYSTEM`, improving performance and reliability over the previous `legacy_code_server_snapshots`.
[SQL Injection Fix] Resolved a critical SQL injection vulnerability in IO managers, requiring all users to upgrade for security.
[DagsterDbt] `enable_source_metadata` now defaults to `True`, enabling automatic remapping of upstream asset keys based on dbt source table names.
[Dagster-AWS] `PickledObjectS3IOManager` now defaults the S3 key prefix to an empty string, simplifying configuration.
[Dagster-Databricks] `run_multi_task` methods now assign unique message destinations to tasks, preventing collisions in concurrent executions.
[UI] Improved asset selection autocomplete performance, enhancing user experience.

analysis.summary: Release 1.13.0 introduces significant enhancements to Dagster's asset management and integration capabilities, particularly with the introduction of virtual assets and improved state management for external integrations. The removal of deprecated features streamlines the API, but requires consumers to update their implementations. Overall, this release aims to improve the developer experience and operational reliability for data pipelines, while enhancing observability and metadata management across integrations.
key_changes: [Virtual Assets] Introduced the `is_virtual` parameter for `@asset` and `AssetSpec`, allowing for the definition of assets that represent views or derived tables without materialization, impacting execution planning and staleness calculations.
[Partitioned Asset Checks] Asset checks can now target specific partitions of upstream assets, aligning data quality checks with partitioned data production and monitoring.
[State Management] State-backed components for integrations like dbt, Fivetran, and Airbyte are now enabled by default, improving the predictability of code location loading.
[Job Configuration] Job-level config defaults are now applied when partial config is provided to a run, enhancing flexibility in job execution.
[CLI Enhancements] Expanded `dg api` commands for programmatic inspection of assets, runs, jobs, and schedules, improving developer tooling.
[Integration Improvements] Enhanced observability and metadata support for integrations with dbt Cloud, Databricks, and others, providing richer operational insights.
[Deprecation Removal] Removed deprecated methods and parameters across various integrations, including `external_asset_from_spec` and `load_component_at_path`, necessitating updates to consumer code.
[Agent Improvements] Enhanced agent failure recovery and more resilient code server redeploy behavior, improving overall system reliability.
breaking_changes: Removed deprecated `external_asset_from_spec` and `external_assets_from_specs`. Use `AssetSpec` inputs directly to `Definitions(...)` or `AssetsDefinition(specs=[...])` instead.
Removed deprecated single-`AssetKey` `deps` argument support from asset dependencies. Use a sequence of `AssetDep` objects instead.
Removed deprecated `get_all_asset_specs` from `Definitions`.
Removed deprecated `legacy_freshness_policy` and `auto_observe_interval_minutes` parameters from `@observable_source_asset`.
Removed deprecated `load_component_at_path` from `ComponentLoadContext`. Use `context.load_component` instead.
Removed deprecated `build_defs_at_path` from `ComponentLoadContext`.
[dagster-airbyte] Removed deprecated `AirbyteState` enum (use `AirbyteJobStatusType` instead) and removed deprecated parameters from `build_airbyte_assets()`.
[dagster-looker] Removed deprecated methods and support from API helpers, including `DagsterLookerResource.build_defs` and `get_asset_key`.
[dagster-powerbi] Removed deprecated `PowerBIWorkspace.build_defs()` and translator key helpers.
[dagster-sigma] Removed deprecated `SigmaOrganization.build_defs()` and related translator support.
analysis.migration_notes: Update all instances of deprecated methods and parameters as specified in the breaking changes. Ensure to replace `external_asset_from_spec` and `load_component_at_path` with their recommended alternatives. Test all asset definitions and integrations to confirm compatibility with the new API.

analysis.summary: Release v1.11.8 addresses several issues related to catalog integration and configuration management, enhancing the dbt compilation process and improving logging for user-defined functions (UDFs). The introduction of the @requires.catalogs decorator for both compile and test commands allows for better support of custom catalog integrations. Additionally, the unpinning of the sqlparse dependency and the introduction of a CLI option for configuring sqlparse limits provide more flexibility for users. Overall, this release improves stability and usability for downstream consumers relying on dbt-core.
key_changes: [Catalog Integration] Added @requires.catalogs decorator to compile and test commands to enhance support for REST Catalog-Linked databases and custom catalog integrations, improving compatibility and functionality.
[Configuration Management] Improved logic for detecting missing plus prefixes in dbt_project.yml, which helps prevent configuration errors and enhances user experience.
[Macros] Introduced config support for macros, allowing meta and docs to coexist under it, which improves documentation capabilities for custom macros.
[Environment Variables] Enabled CLI to pick up environment variables prefixed with DBT_ENGINE, allowing for more flexible configuration in different environments.
[UDFs] Allowed deferral for UDFs, which enhances the flexibility of function definitions and their execution timing.
[Logging] Enhanced logging by resolving full node descriptions while running UDFs, which aids in debugging and monitoring.
[Deprecation Handling] Changed the deprecation warning for invalid config keys in dbt_project.yml to raise a custom key, improving clarity on configuration issues.
[Dependency Management] Unpinned sqlparse dependency and introduced a --sqlparse CLI option for configuring sqlparse limits, allowing users to manage parsing behavior more effectively.
breaking_changes: Deprecated config keys related to property checks that were previously allowed without the plus prefix; users must update their dbt_project.yml to conform to the new requirements.
analysis.migration_notes: Update dbt_project.yml to ensure all config keys are compliant with the new requirements, specifically checking for the presence of the plus prefix where necessary. Test all macros and UDFs to ensure they function correctly with the new logging and configuration changes.

analysis.summary: This release addresses an issue with dbt retry logic for microbatch models, ensuring that the original invocation time is used for recomputing batches instead of the current time. This change improves the accuracy of batch processing and ensures consistency in data outputs. Additionally, the minimum version of dbt-common has been bumped to 1.37.3, which may affect downstream dependencies that rely on this library.
key_changes: [Microbatch Models] Fixed dbt retry logic to use the original invocation time for recomputing batches, enhancing data consistency and correctness.
[Dependencies] Updated minimum version of dbt-common to 1.37.3, which may require consumers to verify compatibility with their existing implementations.
